title: Typed Semantic Definitions Refactor
subtitle: One-off refactor living doc for making living-doc structure and behavior executable without moving concrete docs out of JSON.
scope: Living Doc Compositor registry, templates, MCP diagnostics, skills, generated docs, and contract tests.
sourceCoverage: Seeded from GitHub issue #163 and internal model pages created on 2026-05-05.
objective: Integrate typed semantic definitions into Living Doc Compositor so living-doc structure and behavior have one executable source that generates the registry/template artifacts, relationship graphs, MCP diagnostics, tests, and reference diagrams while concrete living doc instances remain JSON.
successCondition: The refactor is complete when typed definitions generate the selected semantic artifacts deterministically, existing JSON/HTML rendering and sync flows still work, MCP structure reflection uses generated relationship/stage diagnostics, contract tests prevent drift, and at least one template family proves the generated diagnostics add value over the current hand-coded behavior.

issueReferences.title: Add typed semantic definitions for living-doc structure and stage diagnostics

sourceReferences.title: Living Doc Stage Skill Operating Model
Living Doc Knowledge Graph, MCP, and Skill Model
Living Doc Code Representation Value Model

objectiveFacets.name: Target architecture
objectiveFacets.description: Typed definitions become the executable source for reusable semantic machinery while concrete living doc instances remain JSON artifacts.

objectiveFacets.name: Migration path
objectiveFacets.description: The refactor lands through reversible implementation steps that begin with one template family and expand only after proof.

objectiveFacets.name: Behavioral proof
objectiveFacets.description: Generated diagnostics must prove they add relationship or stage insight beyond the current hand-coded local section diagnostics.

objectiveFacets.name: Drift control
objectiveFacets.description: Generated artifacts, MCP behavior, rendered docs, and skill context stay aligned through deterministic generation and contract tests.

objectiveFacets.name: Decision trace
objectiveFacets.description: Key architecture decisions remain explicit while the refactor moves from additive graph metadata toward broader generated semantic machinery.

invariants.name: Concrete docs stay JSON
invariants.statement: Do not move concrete living doc instance content into code; code may define semantic machinery and generate artifacts, but living docs remain portable JSON artifacts.

invariants.name: Prove before expanding
invariants.statement: Do not migrate broad registry or template authorship until one generated template graph improves MCP diagnostics beyond existing structure reflection.

invariants.name: Generated artifacts stay readable
invariants.statement: Generated JSON and docs must remain committed, readable, deterministic, and compatible with the compositor and GitHub Pages workflow.

sections.title: Status Snapshot
sections.rationale: Shows the current stage of the refactor at a glance so the integration objective does not disappear behind detailed architecture, proof, and tooling sections.

callout.title: Current stage
items: The full refactor objective is now integration: typed definitions should become the executable source for semantic machinery while JSON remains the concrete document artifact.
The current stage is still Intent / Seeding because the target is clear but the typed definition source, generated graph, MCP adoption, and proof rungs do not exist yet.

sections.title: Formal Model
sections.rationale: Defines the target semantic model for the refactor: what becomes executable typed machinery, what remains generated artifact, and what remains concrete JSON document state.

data.name: Typed semantic definitions as source of machinery
data.definition: Reusable living-doc semantic machinery is authored as typed definitions for types, templates, expected relationships, stage signals, diagnostics, and operations; generated JSON/metadata remain the artifacts consumed by the compositor, renderer, MCP server, tests, and docs.
data.authorityScope: Registry/template semantics, template relationship graphs, MCP stage diagnostics, valid operations, generated prompt context, and generated diagrams.
notes: This model does not replace concrete living doc JSON instances.
The first definition source should be additive and prove value on one template family.
violations: Moving concrete doc content into code would break the artifact boundary.
Generating artifacts that are not deterministic, readable, or compatible with the compositor would fail the refactor objective.

data.name: Generated template relationship graph
data.definition: A generated graph captures template section order, expected type relationships, stage signals, and valid operations so MCP can reason over relationships rather than only local section shape.
data.authorityScope: Read-only MCP diagnostics and generated documentation for template relationship maps.
notes: The generated graph includes machine-readable stage signal conditions, not only explanatory `when` text.
MCP evaluates those conditions generically for current docs rather than hardcoding template-specific stage logic.
The model now covers two different families: surface delivery and proof/canonicality.

sections.title: Model Assertions
sections.rationale: Keeps the core claims of the refactor explicit so implementation does not proceed on hidden assumptions about JSON, code representation, MCP value, or proof requirements.

data.name: Living doc instances remain JSON artifacts
notes: JSON is still the correct representation for stored living doc state because it is portable, inspectable, patchable, renderable, and archival.

data.name: Code defines behavior over living-doc state
notes: Stage signal behavior now lives in typed definitions as condition objects and is evaluated by MCP generically.
This is the first concrete step where code representation carries behavior, not only documentation or generated metadata.

data.name: MCP diagnostic benefit must be proven before broad migration
notes: The first experiment succeeds only if generated graph diagnostics identify relationship or stage gaps that current section-local diagnostics do not express clearly.

sections.title: Design-Code-Spec Flow
sections.rationale: Tracks the planned implementation path from typed definitions to generated artifacts and MCP adoption, keeping design intent, code targets, and issue scope connected.

data.name: First PR: additive surface-delivery template graph
notes: Initial implementation slice landed: typed definitions for surface-delivery, generated template graph artifact, contract test, MCP template graph tool, and semanticGraph metadata in structure reflection.
Still partial because relationship-gap and stage-diagnostic tools are not implemented yet, and only one template family is covered.

data.name: Extend structure reflection with generated semantic graph
notes: `living_doc_structure_reflect` includes generated semantic graph metadata when the doc matches a known template graph.
Dedicated MCP tools now expose relationship gaps, stage diagnostics, and valid operations for generated template graphs.
Still partial because diagnostics are conservative section/card-population checks; they do not yet inspect rich card-level edges.

sections.title: Code Anchors
sections.rationale: Pins the refactor to the concrete files that currently own registry, templates, MCP behavior, tests, and publishing so the migration stays source-grounded.

data.name: Current registry JSON
data.what_it_does: Defines convergence types, entity types, status sets, prompt guidance, source keys, text fields, and rendering metadata.
why_it_matters: This remains the committed artifact consumed by renderer, compositor, MCP, and GitHub Pages.
Typed definitions may eventually generate selected registry fields, but not in the first implementation step.

data.name: Surface delivery template
data.what_it_does: Provides the first candidate template family for generated relationship graph proof.
why_it_matters: The template has a clear expected chain: status -> design/code/spec flow -> alignment -> verification -> tooling.
That chain is currently described in docs and examples but not executable as generated semantic graph metadata.

data.name: Living doc MCP server
data.what_it_does: Exposes registry, objective, structure, source, coverage, governance, patch, and render tools to inference-time agents.
why_it_matters: The MCP surface already has behavior; the refactor should feed it generated relationship/stage metadata rather than inventing a parallel tool surface.

data.name: Compositor embed sync
data.what_it_does: Embeds registry, templates, profiles, renderer, and helper scripts into the standalone compositor and mirrored docs/scripts assets.
why_it_matters: Generated artifacts must preserve this publishing path; changing source authorship must not break rendered docs or GitHub Pages.

data.name: Typed definition source
data.what_it_does: Defines the surface-delivery semantic graph source: section roles, expected relationships, machine-readable stage signal conditions, and valid operations.
why_it_matters: This is the first executable semantic definition that generates graph metadata instead of hand-maintaining that relationship model only in prose.

data.name: Generated template graph artifact
data.what_it_does: Stores the generated semantic graph consumed by MCP and contract tests.
why_it_matters: Keeps the generated relationship/stage/operation graph inspectable and committed while code remains the source.

data.name: Relationship and stage MCP tools
data.what_it_does: Compares living docs to generated template graph metadata and exposes relationship gaps, likely stage candidates, and valid operations.
why_it_matters: This is the first point where typed semantic definitions produce inference-time behavior rather than only a generated artifact.

data.name: Proof-canonicality typed definition source
data.what_it_does: Defines the proof-canonicality semantic graph source: model/assertion/proof/finding/decision/tooling relationships, stage conditions, and valid operations.
why_it_matters: This second family tests whether the typed semantic graph model generalizes beyond product surface delivery.

data.name: Semantic diagram generator
data.what_it_does: Generates Mermaid relationship diagram source from the generated semantic template graph.
why_it_matters: This is the first generated documentation projection from the typed semantic graph, reducing drift between runtime metadata and explanatory diagrams.

sections.title: Attempt Log
sections.rationale: Records probes and migration attempts as they happen so learning accumulates without prematurely turning every experiment into a decision or reusable template.

data.name: Externalize the operating model into three internal pages
what_tried: Created detailed static pages for stage-aware skills, graph/MCP substrate, and code representation value.
what_proved: The concept can be explained coherently enough to seed a refactor issue and this one-off living doc.
notes: This is a thinking artifact, not proof that the implementation path works.

data.name: Capture refactor as GitHub issue #163
what_tried: Created a phased refactor ticket with first PR scope, MCP additions, generated graph artifact, and validation plan.
what_proved: There is a concrete enough implementation plan to track outside conversation.

data.name: Create one-off refactor living doc before making a template
what_tried: Represented the refactor with existing convergence types rather than adding a reusable refactor template prematurely.
what_proved: Pending: whether this doc shape stays useful during implementation.

data.name: Implement additive typed definition and template graph slice
what_tried: Added typed definition source for surface-delivery, generated graph artifact, contract test, MCP template graph tool, and semantic graph metadata in structure reflection.
what_proved: A typed definition can generate a deterministic relationship/stage/operation graph and expose it through the existing MCP surface without replacing living doc JSON artifacts.
notes: This proves additive integration, not the full relationship-gap diagnostic value yet.

data.name: Expose relationship gaps and stage diagnostics through MCP
what_tried: Added MCP tools for relationship gaps, stage diagnostics, and valid stage operations over generated template graphs.
what_proved: The generated surface-delivery graph can drive inference-time diagnostics beyond raw graph lookup: a synthetic doc now triggers a Coherence-stage gap from flow to alignment.
notes: This is still structural diagnostics, not full semantic edge validation.

data.name: Move stage signal behavior into typed definitions
what_tried: Added condition objects to surface-delivery stage signals and changed MCP stage diagnostics to evaluate condition kinds generically.
what_proved: The typed definition layer can carry executable stage behavior rather than leaving MCP to hardcode template-specific stage logic.
notes: This condition language is deliberately small and should grow only when a second template family needs it.

data.name: Add proof-canonicality as second typed template family
what_tried: Added proof-canonicality typed template definition with relationships, stage signal conditions, and valid operations; regenerated the semantic graph and extended MCP contracts.
what_proved: The generated graph and condition evaluator are not limited to surface delivery; the same mechanism can diagnose assertion/proof gaps in a proof-bearing doc shape.
notes: This still uses structural card population as the evidence of relationship strength; card-level semantic edge validation remains future work.

data.name: Generate template relationship diagrams from semantic graph
what_tried: Added `scripts/generate-living-doc-semantic-diagrams.mjs` and generated `scripts/generated/living-doc-template-diagrams.json` from the semantic graph.
what_proved: The relationship graph can already generate Mermaid diagram source for the covered template families, reducing future hand-maintained diagram drift.
notes: The internal model page references the generated artifact but still keeps richer hand-authored explanatory diagrams for now.

sections.title: Proof Ladder
sections.rationale: Defines the safety and value proof needed before expanding the refactor beyond an additive template graph and one template family.

data.name: Generated template graph is deterministic
notes: `node scripts/generate-living-doc-semantic-graph.mjs --check` is covered by `tests/contract/template-graphs.spec.mjs`.

data.name: Generated graph finds a relationship gap current reflection does not name
notes: `living_doc_relationship_gaps` detects missing or weak expected relationships from generated template graph metadata.
`living_doc_stage_diagnostics` derives stage candidates from machine-readable condition objects in the generated graph.
Contracts now prove this for both surface-delivery (`flow-feeds-alignment`) and proof-canonicality (`assertion-requires-proof`).

data.name: Existing contract and render tests remain green
notes: Targeted MCP contract passes with generated graph lookup, relationship gap diagnostics, stage diagnostics, and valid stage operations.
Full contract suite still needs to be run after this doc update.

sections.title: Investigation Findings
sections.rationale: Separates observed facts about the current system from implementation claims and decisions, especially where the existing MCP surface already solves part of the problem.

data.name: The MCP surface is already richer than raw JSON reads
notes: `scripts/living-doc-mcp-server.mjs` already exposes registry, structure, sources, coverage, governance, patch, and render tools.
The refactor should improve the existing MCP behavior with generated relationship/stage metadata rather than create a replacement surface.

data.name: The current gap is relational and stage awareness
notes: Current reflection checks sections locally and governance broadly, but template relationship chains and stage inference are not yet first-class generated machinery.

sections.title: Decision Record
sections.rationale: Preserves architecture decisions that should constrain the refactor, including the JSON artifact boundary and the additive-first migration strategy.

data.name: Do not move living doc instances out of JSON
notes: Typed code definitions are for semantic machinery; concrete living docs remain JSON artifacts.

data.name: Start with additive generated graph metadata
notes: The first PR should not rewrite the registry or existing template JSON.

data.name: Use surface-delivery as the first proof family unless implementation reveals a better fit
notes: Surface delivery has a clear relationship chain and should expose whether generated graph diagnostics add practical value.

sections.title: Tooling Surface
sections.rationale: Tracks the generator, contract tests, MCP commands, and render/sync tools that must exist or remain stable for the refactor to be operational.

data.name: Semantic graph generator
data.purpose: Generate template relationship graph metadata from typed semantic definitions.
data.whenToUse: Run after changing typed definitions, and in contract tests to verify generated artifact determinism.
caveats: Covers surface-delivery and proof-canonicality so far.
Generates additive graph metadata only; it does not generate registry or template JSON yet.
Stage conditions are intentionally small and structural: section-empty, related-relationship-gap, source-populated-target-empty, all-populated-no-high-gaps, and manual-review.

data.name: Template graph contract test
data.purpose: Ensure generated graph and diagram metadata is deterministic and references valid registry/template types and relationships.
data.whenToUse: Run as part of `npm run test:contract` after the generator exists.
caveats: Validates deterministic generation and registry/template references for surface-delivery and proof-canonicality.
Will need stronger assertions as generated diagnostics move from section/card population toward richer card-level semantic edges.

data.name: Existing living-doc MCP server
data.purpose: Expose living-doc registry, structure, source, coverage, governance, patch, and render operations to inference-time agents.
data.whenToUse: Use as the integration point for generated relationship/stage metadata.
caveats: `living_doc_structure_reflect` includes semantic graph metadata for matching docs.
Relationship diagnostics are intentionally conservative and do not yet validate card-level semantic edges.
